tasks.label: My share of the group project

memory.summary: Teammate_A dumped their work on me again, but arguing is too much hassle. I'll just finish it quickly to avoid conflict and get back to my own stuff.

desires: Finish the dumped work immediately to stop the interpersonal friction and get back to my own schedule
Avoid any confrontation or awkwardness with teammate_A by just doing what they want
Get the task done fast so I can ignore it and focus on my own priorities without thinking about it

goals.description: Immediately complete teammate_A's dumped portion of the project to eliminate current interpersonal friction and avoid confrontation.
goals.rationale: My conflict-avoidant nature makes the awkwardness of saying 'no' or negotiating unacceptable right now. The immediate social discomfort is a higher priority than my own schedule. I am impulsive, so I'm choosing the path of least resistance (doing it) rather than planning a future negotiation.

goals.description: Finish both parts of the project by 16:00 to free up the rest of the afternoon for my own priorities without lingering guilt.
goals.rationale: I value academic/task outcomes highly, but only insofar as they clear the deck. My short planning horizon means I want to 'get this over with' immediately so I can mentally check out and focus on my own goals later, ignoring the extra work entirely once done.

goals.description: Ignore the fact that I just took on double work and do not reflect on the unfairness or long-term consequences of this pattern.
goals.rationale: To maintain my avoidance strategy, I must suppress any internal narrative about being taken advantage of. Focusing on the injustice would create cognitive dissonance and potential conflict; ignoring it allows me to stay compliant and move on quickly.

agent.last_reasoning: Advances g1 (avoid conflict) and g2 (finish by 16:00). I ignore the unfairness (g3) to keep stress low. Short-term focus means doing it now rather than negotiating later.
